description: A dedicated coding-task runtime module can assemble the store, manager, launcher, monitor, recovery helper, and notifier from one workspace/config root.
steps: Instantiate the runtime helper for a temporary workspace and verify it returns the expected coding-task collaborators.
Confirm every collaborator points at the same workspace-scoped automation path rather than creating divergent task stores.
Run focused coding-task tests and verify the new runtime assembly does not break existing persistence behavior.

description: The runtime helper can be created both with and without an outbound notifier callback so CLI-only paths do not need ad hoc partial wiring.
steps: Build the coding-task runtime without a message bus callback.
Verify create/list/status style commands still have access to store, manager, launcher, and monitor without constructing extra helper objects.
Build the same runtime with an outbound callback and confirm notifier support is available for gateway paths.

description: The active CLI gateway entrypoint uses the shared coding-task runtime builder instead of manually wiring coding-task collaborators inline.
steps: Start the gateway in a controlled test harness.
Verify startup still reports tracked and recoverable coding-task counts.
Confirm the gateway path no longer assembles launcher, monitor, recovery, and notifier by hand in the command body.

description: CLI coding-task commands reuse the shared runtime instead of instantiating launcher or monitor ad hoc inside individual commands.
steps: Run create, list, status, resume, cancel, and run against a temporary workspace.
Verify the commands still succeed and report the same task data as before.
Inspect the command implementations and confirm they all resolve collaborators through the shared runtime path.

description: AgentLoop command interception reuses the same coding-task runtime wiring contract instead of reconstructing launcher and monitor privately.
steps: Instantiate AgentLoop with coding-task support enabled in a focused test.
Verify Telegram coding-task command interception still works for start, status, stop, cancel, and resume.
Confirm AgentLoop no longer constructs a separate launcher/monitor stack outside the shared runtime contract.

description: Coding-task task-selection and concurrency rules live in a dedicated policy layer rather than being hardcoded in the Telegram router.
steps: Create tasks from different origins in a focused test fixture.
Verify the policy chooses the same active task as today's behavior for the current MVP rules.
Confirm the router delegates selection and single-active enforcement to the policy helper instead of embedding those rules inline.

description: The extracted coding-task policy preserves the current single-active-task MVP rule for Telegram private chat.
steps: Start one coding task from Telegram private chat in a focused integration test.
Attempt to start a second task from the same workspace while the first stays non-terminal.
Verify the same rejection behavior and user-facing explanation are preserved after the policy extraction.

description: The extracted coding-task policy preserves current origin-chat task selection for Telegram control commands.
steps: Create multiple coding tasks associated with different Telegram chats.
Send `状态` or `继续` from one chat in a focused test.
Verify nanobot still targets the newest non-terminal task for that origin chat only.

description: Progress reporting exposes a pure read path that builds task reports without persisting metadata or mutating task state.
steps: Call the report-building API for a task with existing repo metadata and pane output.
Verify the returned report includes branch, commit, harness-note, and live-output summaries.
Confirm the task record on disk is unchanged by the pure read call.

description: A dedicated refresh path can explicitly persist repo metadata and progress summaries when polling or lifecycle code needs to update task state.
steps: Invoke the refresh API for a running or starting task with new pane output.
Verify branch metadata, recent commit summary, and progress summary are persisted.
Confirm waiting-user detection and other lifecycle updates happen only through the explicit refresh path.

description: CLI `coding-task status` uses the pure read/report path for display and no longer mutates task state just because a user viewed status.
steps: Create a coding task with known metadata and capture its persisted state before running the status command.
Run `nanobot coding-task status <id>`.
Verify the output still includes branch, commit, and live report details while the underlying task record is unchanged unless an explicit refresh ran earlier.

description: Gateway polling and recovery use the explicit refresh path so state mutation stays localized to background lifecycle code.
steps: Run the focused recovery and notifier test paths.
Verify polling still refreshes persisted progress and repo metadata for active tasks.
Confirm the gateway watch loop does not rely on status-view side effects to keep task state current.

description: Telegram `状态` reporting keeps the current user-facing summary while reading from the new pure report path.
steps: Trigger Telegram `状态` in a focused routing test for an active coding task.
Verify the reply still includes task id, status, repo, recent progress, and live report content.
Confirm the reply path does not rely on hidden persistence side effects from a read-only report builder.

description: Focused regression tests cover the new runtime builder, policy layer, and pure-report versus refresh split.
steps: Run the focused coding-task test files that cover runtime assembly, routing, progress, recovery, notifier, and CLI status behavior.
Verify the new tests would fail if runtime assembly drift or report-side mutation were reintroduced.
Confirm the test suite remains explicitly scoped away from the unrelated repo_sync baseline failure.

description: Repository documentation explains the refactored coding-task runtime boundaries so future sessions know where to extend orchestration behavior.
steps: Open the updated architecture documentation or module docstrings.
Verify it states where runtime assembly, policy, report building, and lifecycle refresh now live.
Confirm the docs do not reintroduce the old duplicated wiring story.

description: A focused end-to-end smoke path still supports create, run, and status for coding tasks after the architectural cleanup.
steps: Create a coding task in a temporary workspace against a local repo fixture.
Launch the worker and inspect status through the refactored runtime path.
Verify nanobot still surfaces the same task identity, harness state, and live report shape after the refactor.
